comments.comment: so the 10% comes from the campaign points they earn? is there a cap on how many people i can refer or is it uncapped?
comments.reply: No cap on referrals, and yes, it's a cut of their campaign points based on work.

comments.comment: idk man, "mathematically impossible to rug" is exactly what a rug pull would say. color me skeptical until i see the audits.
comments.reply: Fair reaction. That's why I asked about the contracts. Check the link, the code is open.

comments.comment: i’ve been burned by so many "passive income" loops in defi. usually the gas fees to claim end up costing more than the rewards. hoping this is different.
comments.reply: Gas is minimal on the layer, which is why I was skeptical at first too. But it works.

comments.comment: finally someone building on genlayer that isn't just a demo. the attribution tech is actually insane if it works as advertised.
comments.reply: It's wild what they're building on that infra. Attribution is the missing link.

comments.comment: has anyone actually verified the genlayer integration? looking at the abi right now but want a second pair of eyes.
comments.reply: The GenLayer integration handles the verification. You can trace the state changes.

comments.comment: i audited the contracts last week. the logic is actually sound, the 10% split is hardcoded in the campaign factory.
comments.reply: Glad to hear someone else dug into the code! It's solid.

comments.comment: how are the points actually valued though? do we know what 1 point equals in dollar terms yet or is it tba?
comments.reply: Points are currently for the campaign, but the on-chain data means they have inherent value for future distribution.

comments.comment: forever models never work. eventually the flow of new users stops and the bottom tier gets squeezed. how is this different?
comments.reply: Because it's tied to *value creation* (work), not just new users joining. It's affiliate marketing 2.0.

comments.comment: this reminds me of the early days of friend.tech referrals. hot at first, then everyone left. hope the "work" part keeps it alive.
comments.reply: The 'work' aspect is the key differentiator here. It's not just social volume.

comments.comment: just checked the link. seeing the points stack on-chain in real time is actually kind of addicting ngl.
comments.reply: Right? Watching the counter go up without a middleman taking a cut feels good.

comments.comment: "earn forever" sounds great but what happens to my points if a creator i referred stops creating? do they expire?
comments.reply: The points you earned up to that point are yours. The 'forever' part applies as long as they are active.

comments.comment: is the attribution logic handled entirely on-chain or is there an off-chain oracle feeding the ai data to genlayer?
comments.reply: It's hybrid. GenLayer validates the attribution logic, but the points settle on-chain.

comments.comment: been using it for a few days. the transparency on where points come from is a total game changer compared to standard airdrops.
comments.reply: The transparency is what won me over. You can see exactly where the value comes from.

comments.comment: most referral programs are just ponzis in disguise. nice to see one tied to actual creator output rather than just speculation.
comments.reply: Exactly. Tying it to real output removes the ponzi element.

comments.comment: if this actually cuts out the grifters, i'm all in. tired of clicking buttons for zero value.
comments.reply: No more grift. Just proof of work.

comments.comment: ai-verified attribution sounds like buzzword soup to me. who verifies the verifier?
comments.reply: It's GenLayer, so the verification logic is trustless execution. Pretty wild tech.

comments.comment: do i need to hold any tokens to start earning or is it just purely based on the referral activity?
comments.reply: Purely referral/activity based. No token holding required to participate.
